name: Bujdal ibn Sulaym
title: Thief of the Ring
shortDescription: One of the criminals of Karbala who severed Imam Hussain's (AS) holy finger to steal his ring.
fullStory:
Bujdal ibn Sulaym al-Kalbi is the symbol of baseness and greed in the Ashura event. He was a soldier in Umar Sa'd's army who went into the slaughter pit to loot the martyrs' bodies.

When Imam Hussain's (AS) holy body lay blood-drenched on the ground and everyone was stealing a piece of his clothes or weapons, Bujdal noticed a ring on the Imam's hand.

The blood had dried and the ring wouldn't come off easily. This ruthless executioner, instead of giving up, used his sword (or dagger) to sever the holy finger of the Master of Martyrs (AS) just to steal the ring.

This heinous crime angered Mukhtar al-Thaqafi. After Mukhtar's uprising, when Bujdal was captured, Mukhtar ordered his hands and feet to be amputated, leaving him to bleed out and die in agony.
quoteArabic: فَلَمّا لَمْ يَخْرُجِ الخاتَمُ... قَطَعَ إِصْبَعَهُ...
quote: (The painful Maqtal narrative:) And when the ring did not come off... he severed his finger...
